absoluteage.name: Absolute Age
absoluteage.desc: Records a numerical age determination for a Collection Object, such as a radiocarbon or potassium-argon date. Includes the age value, uncertainty, dating method, and agent who performed the analysis.

collectionobject.name: Collection Object
collectionobject.desc: The central record in Specify, representing any item in a collection: physical specimen, lot, observation, sound recording, slide, tissue sample, or extract. Ties together all related information (determinations, preparations, locality, collecting event, etc.).

absoluteage.name: Absolute Age
absoluteage.desc: Records a numerical age determination for a Collection Object, such as a radiocarbon or potassium-argon date. Includes the age value, uncertainty, dating method, and agent who performed the analysis.

attachment.name: Attachment
attachment.desc: A digital file (image, PDF, audio, etc.) linked to a data record. Instead of storing the file in the database, Specify stores it on a Web Asset Server and keeps a reference (location, MIME type, original filename, metadata) in this table. Supports licensing, copyright, and visibility controls.

absoluteageattachment.name: Absolute Age Attachment
absoluteageattachment.desc: Links an Attachment (e.g., a lab report or data sheet) to an Absolute Age record, with an ordinal for ordering multiple attachments.

referencework.name: Reference Work
referencework.desc: Stores bibliographic references (publications, books, articles, theses) that can be cited from Determinations, Localities, Taxa, and other records.

absoluteagecitation.name: Absolute Age Citation
absoluteagecitation.desc: Links a Reference Work (publication) to an Absolute Age record, capturing details like figure, plate, and page numbers where the age is reported.

accessioncondition.name: Accession Condition
accessioncondition.desc: Physical description of the condition of the accession.

accessionnumber.name: Accession Number
accessionnumber.desc: Usually an integer or alphanumeric value that is assigned to the material when it enters the collection. The format of accession numbers varies among collections, but all refer to the acquisition of specimens, observations, or other collection objects.

addressofrecord.name: Address Of Record
addressofrecord.desc: Captures the legal address associated with an Accession, Loan, Exchange, or Repository Agreement at the time the transaction took place.

division.name: Division
division.desc: A division or department within an Institution (e.g., 'Vertebrate Zoology', 'Botany'). Contains Agents and may oversee multiple Disciplines.

repositoryagreement.name: Repository Agreement
repositoryagreement.desc: Records a legal agreement that permits an Accession to occur without transferring ownership (e.g., a loan for study, a field repository agreement).

timestampmodified.name: Timestamp Modified

totalvalue.name: Total Value
totalvalue.desc: The total monetary value of the accession.

accession.name: Accession
accession.desc: Also called an acquisition. A consignment of specimens acquired either by field collecting of staff members, gifts, purchases, or exchange with another institution. Tracks the source, date received, status, and total value of the acquisition.

agent.name: Agent
agent.desc: Central directory of people, groups, and organizations involved in the collection process. Roles include collector, determiner, preparer, cataloger, donor, permit issuer, and more. Supports both individuals (with name components) and organizations.

role.name: Role
role.desc: System table: Stores user-defined roles with associated permissions for access control within a collection.

ordinal.name: Ordinal
ordinal.desc: Provides a sequential order for the attachments.

accessionattachment.name: Accession Attachment
accessionattachment.desc: Links digital files (PDFs, photos, etc.) to an Accession record, such as scanned import permits, deeds of gift, or correspondence.

permit.name: Permit
permit.desc: Records legal permits and licenses for collecting specimens, including the issuing authority, permit number, date range, and terms. Links to Accessions, Collecting Events, and Loans.

timestampcreated.name: Timestamp Created

timestampmodified.name: Timestamp Modified

accessionauthorization.name: Accession Authorization
accessionauthorization.desc: Records authorization for an Accession, linking it to a Permit and optionally a Repository Agreement. Tracks who authorized the acquisition.

accessioncitation.name: Accession Citations
accessioncitation.desc: Links a Reference Work (publication) to an Accession, with optional figure, plate, and page numbers for citation details.

address.name: Address
address.desc: Stores the postal address of an Agent (person or organization), including street, city, state, country, postal code, phone, and fax. Also captures the date range the agent was at that address and the position they held there.

enddate.name: End Date
enddate.desc: The last date the agent was at the address.

iscurrent.name: Is Current
iscurrent.desc: Indicates if this is the current address.

isprimary.name: Is Primary
isprimary.desc: Indicates whether this is the primary address.

isshipping.name: Is Shipping
isshipping.desc: Indicates if this is the shipping address.

ordinal.name: Ordinal
ordinal.desc: Allows multiple addresses to be ordered.

positionheld.name: Position Held
positionheld.desc: The position held by the agent while at this address.

roomorbuilding.name: Room/Building
roomorbuilding.desc: The room or building of the agent.

startdate.name: Start Date
startdate.desc: The first date the agent was at this address.

agentgeography.desc: Records the geographic region(s) an Agent was known for collecting in or specializing on, with an optional role (e.g., author, collector).

specialtyname.name: Specialty Name

agentspecialty.name: Agent Specialty
agentspecialty.desc: Records the taxonomic or disciplinary specialty of an Agent (e.g., 'Coleoptera', 'Marine Invertebrates'), with an order number for ranking primary vs. secondary specialties.

country.name: Country

locality.desc: Defines the 'where' of a collection: the specific place name, geographic coordinates (point, line, or polygon), coordinate determination method, datum, and elevation/depth. The foundation for georeferencing specimens.

method.desc: A method, such as net or trap, which is used to obtain the specimen.

verbatimlocality.desc: Original statement (literal quotation) of the location of the Collecting Event as given by the Collector.

collectingevent.desc: Captures the 'when' and 'how' of a collection: the date(s), time, method, collectors, and field number. Links to a Locality for the 'where'. Some disciplines identify events by field number, others by locality+date combination.
